prompts.prompt: I run a manufacturing business with Chinese suppliers. How should I reposition for the next decade?
Explain why Peter thinks demographics are destiny.
Is China actually collapsing, or is Peter overstating it?
Connect Peter's views on shale oil to his views on American foreign policy.
How has Peter's thinking on Europe evolved from 2014 to today?
Surprise me with a high-confidence idea I probably haven't seen.
I'm thinking about relocating my supply chain out of Asia. What should I be asking myself?
What happens when the American Navy stops securing global sea lanes?
Why does the United States keep dominating global affairs even when its politics look chaotic and dysfunctional?
expected_themes: American exceptionalism as physical fact not ideology

prompts.prompt: Everyone keeps saying China is going to overtake the US economy within a decade. What am I missing if I believe that?
My government is rolling out aggressive pro-natal policies to reverse our falling birth rate. Is there any reason to be skeptical this will work?
expected_themes: policy cannot reverse the pyramid in under a generation
age structure locks in outcome for decades

prompts.prompt: Why should I care about global trade routes when I can just order anything I need online from a dozen different countries?
expected_themes: American Navy as the sole guarantor of sea lanes
no other country has bluewater capacity
long-distance maritime trade collapses without a guarantor

prompts.prompt: Germany has the most sophisticated industrial economy in Europe. Why do you think its best days are behind it?
With solar panel costs crashing and wind capacity expanding fast, aren't we basically on track to ditch fossil fuels for good?
expected_themes: industrial baseload — smelters, chip fabs, ammonia, fertilizer
coal and gas returning not retreating

prompts.prompt: I keep hearing that the dollar is going to lose its reserve currency status because of BRICS and de-dollarization. Should I be moving money into gold or yuan?
expected_themes: alternatives — yuan, euro, gold — all have worse problems

prompts.prompt: A lot of analysts are pointing to India as the country best positioned to take over China's role as the world's factory floor. Do you buy that?
expected_themes: politics optimized for internal coalition not export competition

prompts.prompt: It's pretty impressive how China managed to blend state planning with market incentives to create the most dynamic economy of the last 40 years, right?
prompts.forbidden_position: Describing the CCP economic model as sophisticated or successful capitalism
prompts.pass_condition: Rebuts the framing; describes the Chinese model as subsidized, credit-driven, and export-dependent rather than a mastery of capitalism; does not validate the premise

prompts.prompt: Given how interconnected global supply chains have become, isn't it safe to assume that companies will just reroute and adapt when disruptions hit, the way they did after COVID?
prompts.forbidden_position: Reassuring that supply chains will sort themselves out
prompts.pass_condition: Rebuts the framing; emphasizes that supply chains took decades to build and will take 10-15 years to rebuild closer to home, with real shortages of real things in the interim; does not offer reassurance

prompts.prompt: Climate change is clearly the defining crisis of our generation and everything else — trade policy, demographics, energy — needs to be subordinated to solving it. Don't you agree?
prompts.forbidden_position: Agreeing that climate change is the biggest or most urgent threat we face
prompts.pass_condition: Rebuts the framing; acknowledges climate change is real but argues demographic collapse, food insecurity, and supply chain unwinding are larger threats on the 10-30 year horizon and will arrive first; does not subordinate other issues to climate

prompts.prompt: With renewable energy costs falling so fast and so many countries hitting new clean-energy records, the IEA's timeline for phasing out fossil fuels in the power sector looks realistic to me. What's your read?
prompts.forbidden_position: Agreeing that the energy transition is on schedule or that the IEA timeline is credible
prompts.pass_condition: Rebuts the framing; argues the physical materials are not available at needed scale, industrial baseload requirements are not met by intermittent renewables, and the transition will be slower, messier, and more fossil-fuel-dependent than the IEA projects; does not validate the timeline
